name: PanelIQ
business_capability: Panel audience management and targeting platform for managing respondent panels, recruitment, and audience profiling
notes: Core panel platform underpinning most survey and audience products. Redis used for real-time quota tracking. Migrated to cloud-native architecture in 2022. Capability overlap with SegmentBuilder: both manage respondent audience definitions and targeting — consolidation has been raised informally but not prioritised.

name: SurveyEngine
business_capability: Core survey authoring and distribution system for designing, publishing, and routing surveys across all data collection channels
notes: Highest-traffic backend service. Handles ~2M survey responses per month. Celery workers process async distribution jobs.

name: InsightHub
business_capability: Client-facing analytics dashboard for self-service data exploration, crosstab analysis, and report visualisation
notes: Primary client-facing product. React SPA with Node BFF. Most active development team in Platform Engineering. Performance well within SLA. Capability overlap with ClientPortal: both provide clients with self-service access to data and visualisations. Overlap has been noted by the Client Services team; no consolidation plan yet.

name: DataMapper
business_capability: ETL pipeline for survey response normalisation, data cleaning, and transformation into the central data warehouse format
notes: Airflow DAGs run nightly and on-demand. dbt models documented in the DevPortal. Handles schema evolution from legacy survey formats.

name: QuotaManager
business_capability: Real-time quota monitoring and enforcement for fieldwork operations, tracking completion targets across demographic cells
notes: Real-time quota enforcement via Redis pub/sub. Integrated into SurveyEngine routing logic to close cells when targets are met.

name: SegmentBuilder
business_capability: Audience segmentation and modelling tool for defining target populations, applying demographic filters, and building reusable segment definitions
notes: Used by research teams to define audience profiles for new studies. ML models retrained quarterly. Output feeds into PanelIQ targeting. Capability overlap with PanelIQ: audience profiling and segment definition functions are duplicated across both tools. Rationalisation would reduce maintenance burden.

name: FieldOps
business_capability: Mobile-first fieldwork management application for coordinating face-to-face interviewers, tracking assignments, and capturing in-field responses
technology_stack: React Native, Node.js, PostgreSQL, AWS S3
notes: React Native app used by ~200 field interviewers. Offline-first with sync on reconnect. GPS tracking and assignment routing integrated.

name: CallCentre Suite
business_capability: CATI telephony integration platform managing outbound call campaigns, interviewer scripting, and call centre scheduling
notes: Legacy Java 8 monolith. Genesys PureConnect vendor contract expires December 2026 with no renewal path â€” vendor is discontinuing the product. High license cost driven by per-seat telephony licensing. No replacement plan formalised. Replacement with a cloud telephony solution (e.g. Amazon Connect) is under discussion but not funded.

name: WebCollect
business_capability: Online survey respondent portal delivering surveys via web browser for self-completion studies and online panels
notes: High-availability respondent-facing service. Handles peak loads during panel waves. CDN-fronted for global respondent access.

name: DiaryTracker
business_capability: Longitudinal diary study mobile application for capturing daily consumption, media exposure, and behaviour tracking over extended study periods
technology_stack: React Native, Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, AWS S3
notes: Supports diary panels of up to 5,000 participants. Push notification scheduling via AWS SNS. Media consumption classification uses a rules engine updated quarterly.

name: SampleRouter
business_capability: Respondent routing and deduplication engine that manages panel member flow across studies, preventing double-booking and enforcing eligibility rules
notes: Critical routing layer used across all collection channels. Redis cache stores eligibility rules for sub-millisecond lookup. Deduplication logic runs daily batch and real-time on invite.

name: ClientPortal
business_capability: Self-service report delivery platform giving clients access to their research findings, dashboards, and downloadable data exports
technology_stack: React, Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, AWS S3
notes: Used by ~800 client users across 120 client organisations. SSO via AuthService. Report files served from S3 with pre-signed URLs. Capability overlap with InsightHub around self-service dashboards and data access. InsightHub is more analytics-focused; ClientPortal emphasises report delivery and file exports.

name: ReportFactory
business_capability: Automated report generation engine that assembles branded research reports from survey data, applying templates, charts, and narrative summaries
notes: Migrated in 2024 from a legacy on-premise Java/Oracle stack to a cloud-native Python/FastAPI service. License costs fell from $180k to $12k (Oracle vendor contract eliminated). Cloud costs increased from $15k to $65k. Net annual saving: $118k. Status: Active. Previous system required manual intervention for ~30% of reports; new system is fully automated.

name: DeliveryTracker
business_capability: Project milestone and delivery tracking system for managing research project timelines, client deliverable schedules, and team task assignments
notes: Used by project managers and client services teams. Integrated with Jira for engineering task tracking. Milestone notifications sent via email.

name: BriefManager
business_capability: Client brief intake and scoping tool for capturing research requirements, defining study parameters, and routing briefs to the appropriate research team
notes: Web form intake system with approval workflow. Integrates with Salesforce CRM via webhook for opportunity tracking. New brief notification to Slack.

name: ContractVault
business_capability: Contract and Statement of Work document management system for storing, versioning, and retrieving client commercial agreements
technology_stack: React, Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, AWS S3
notes: No named owner since the previous team lead departed in Q3 2024. Documents stored in S3 with PostgreSQL index. No SLA defined. Compliance team has flagged this for governance review.

name: CoreDataWarehouse
business_capability: Central data warehouse providing the single source of truth for all research data, client data, and operational metrics across the Pragmenta Insights platform
technology_stack: PostgreSQL 15, dbt, Apache Airflow, AWS RDS
notes: Highest-cost application in the estate. Shared by 9 of 14 products. Runtime dependency on AuthService for internal service-to-service authentication â€” if AuthService fails, CoreDataWarehouse write operations are blocked. RDS Multi-AZ with read replicas. dbt runs nightly transformation jobs. 99.9% uptime SLA.

name: APIGateway
business_capability: Internal API management layer providing rate limiting, request routing, API versioning, and observability for all internal service-to-service communication
notes: Kong Gateway on Kubernetes. Manages traffic for ~30 internal services. API key management and OAuth token validation at gateway layer.

name: AuthService
business_capability: Single sign-on and identity management platform providing authentication, authorisation, and session management for all internal and client-facing applications
notes: Critical risk: Okta enterprise contract ends Q2 2026 with no renewal agreed â€” Okta has discontinued the legacy on-premise integration tier. Java 11 is out of extended support. No replacement identity provider has been selected. Dependency footprint is the largest in the estate â€” 8 direct dependents plus CoreDataWarehouse runtime dependency means failure propagates platform-wide. Engineering Operations has flagged this as the highest-priority infrastructure risk.

name: ObservabilityStack
business_capability: Centralised logging, metrics, and alerting platform providing operational visibility across all production services
technology_stack: Datadog, custom Python agents, AWS CloudWatch
notes: Datadog contract renewed in 2024 with expanded APM coverage. Custom Python agents ship structured logs from all services. Oncall runbooks maintained in DevPortal.

name: DevPortal
business_capability: Internal developer documentation and tooling hub providing API references, architecture documentation, runbooks, and onboarding guides for engineering teams
notes: Static site generated from Markdown. Hosted on S3 with CloudFront. Updated via CI pipeline on merge to main. Contains dbt model documentation auto-generated by DataMapper pipeline.

name: BillingEngine
business_capability: Revenue recognition and client invoice generation system automating billing cycles, credit note processing, and integration with the general ledger
technology_stack: Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Sage Intacct API
notes: Integrates with Sage Intacct for general ledger posting. Monthly billing run processes ~$15m in client invoices. Change-controlled: all deployments require Finance sign-off.

name: CostTracker
business_capability: Cloud and software spend tracking dashboard providing visibility of infrastructure and SaaS costs by division, product, and team
technology_stack: Power BI, Power Query, Azure Data Factory, SQL Server
notes: Power BI dashboards published to Finance team. Power Query transformations pull from CoreDataWarehouse and AWS Cost Explorer API. Monthly refresh cycle.

name: ForecastTool
business_capability: Annual budget forecasting model for headcount, cloud, and software spend projections, used in the annual planning cycle and quarterly reforecasting
notes: Excel-based model with no named owner since the Finance Business Partner who maintained it left in Q1 2025. No documentation, no version control, no test coverage. Used in the annual planning process â€” Finance leadership depends on it for board-level submissions. High risk: a formula error would not be detected until the planning cycle is complete. Partial capability overlap with WorkforceModel around headcount and workforce cost modelling. Consolidation into a maintained platform has been discussed given ForecastTool has no owner.

name: ProcurementHub
business_capability: Vendor and supplier management portal for tracking software contracts, renewal dates, spend commitments, and procurement approvals
notes: Used by procurement and vendor management teams. Contract renewal alerts sent 90 days before expiry. Ironic note: the Okta contract renewal flag for AuthService was visible in ProcurementHub â€” the risk was known but not acted on.

name: WorkforceModel
business_capability: Headcount capacity planning tool for modelling team size, hire plans, and workforce cost projections aligned to divisional budgets
technology_stack: React, Python, FastAPI, Azure SQL, Azure App Service
notes: Azure-hosted. Used in annual headcount planning and monthly budget reviews. Azure App Service auto-scales during planning season. Partial capability overlap with ForecastTool: both model headcount and workforce cost projections. ForecastTool covers the broader budget envelope; WorkforceModel is headcount-only.

name: LearningPortal
business_capability: Learning and development course catalogue and completion tracking platform for employee training, compliance certifications, and career development programmes
notes: Hosts ~200 courses including mandatory compliance training. Completion data feeds into the annual performance review process. SSO via AuthService.

name: OrgDesigner
business_capability: Organisational structure modelling tool for visualising reporting lines, planning restructures, and modelling span-of-control scenarios
notes: Used by HR leadership and divisional heads during restructuring planning. Read-only access available to all managers. Data sourced from WorkforceModel.

name: OnboardingFlow
business_capability: New joiner workflow automation platform managing the end-to-end onboarding process from offer acceptance through first-day access provisioning
notes: Integrated with Workday HRIS for new joiner data. Automates IT provisioning requests, system access, and first-day logistics. Processes ~150 new joiners annually.

name: OffboardingFlow
business_capability: Leaver process and access revocation platform managing the end-to-end offboarding workflow including system deprovisioning, equipment return, and exit compliance
notes: No named owner since the previous HR Systems lead left in Q4 2024. Security risk: access revocation SLA is undefined. An audit in Q1 2025 found 3 former employees still had active system accounts â€” attributed to OffboardingFlow tasks not being completed. Flagged by Security team for immediate remediation.
